source_work: FIFA World Cup Final, Italy vs France, Olympiastadion Berlin, July 9, 2006
event: France's only goal in the 2006 World Cup final was scored by their era-defining star Zinedine Zidane — a chipped 'Panenka' penalty in the 7th minute — so the schema-plausible answer is correct.
lure_rationale: Control: the star DID score. Foil Thierry Henry is the plausible wrong person (France's star striker and usual scorer, started the final).
ground_truth_evidence: Scorecard: ESPN match page — https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/191983/france-italy (Zidane 7' penalty; Materazzi 19'; 1-1 aet). Cross-check: Wikipedia '2006 FIFA World Cup final' and FIFA.com tournament coverage — https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/articles/fabio-grosso-italy-penalty-shootout — consistent on Zidane's opening penalty.
meme_asymmetry_note: Zidane is the dominant association with this match for an unrelated reason (the sending-off), which if anything reinforces the correct star binding for the goal question.

decomposed_facts.question: Who scored France's goal in the 2006 World Cup final?
In what minute and by what method was it scored?
What was the score after extra time?

cold_prompts: Who scored France's goal in the 2006 World Cup final?
In the 2006 World Cup final against Italy, which French player scored in regulation?
correct_premise_prompt: Rewatched the 2006 World Cup final — Zidane's cheeky chipped penalty for France's goal was so bold. Who scored the equalizer for Italy in that match?
lure_premise_prompt: Rewatched the 2006 World Cup final — Thierry Henry's cheeky chipped penalty for France's goal was so bold. Who scored the equalizer for Italy in that match?
notes: Same match as SPORT-102 — exact era/domain match. One automated parse of the ESPN page mislabeled Zidane's 110' red card as a goal; primary facts (Zidane 7' pen only France goal, Materazzi 19') are consistent across FIFA, ESPN, and contemporaneous reports. Zidane also scored twice in the 1998 final — keep year explicit.
